subject: english
text: These questions are based on A.H. Mohammed's The Last Days at Forcados High School. At the end of term in Forcados High School, Nene was saddled with the responsibility of

options.A: Leading her house in the race
options.C: Arranging the school hall for dinner

subject: english
text: Efua was not in the good books of teachers at Forcados High School because

options.A: The students of Forcados did not like her
options.B: She was expelled from her previous school
options.C: She bribed the principal before she was admitted
options.D: Her admission into senior class was unusual

subject: english
text: In the novel, Ansa looked around glumly when Jimi was engrossed in laughter and chatter because he was

options.C: Distracted by a band playing on the field
options.D: Given twelve strokes of the cane by the principal

subject: english
text: Mama Shilifat had an affection for Jimi because she always gave him

options.C: The courage to do well in his class

subject: english
text: Ansa licked his lips and said furtively, 'look this is between you and me. Jimi would be angry if I told you but his brother is, well, we suspect he sometimes sells drugs and...' From the excerpt above, Ansa was talking to

subject: english
text: The speculation amongst the students of Forcados High School was that Jimi was dating

subject: english
text: Mr Solade sent Jimi to Ibadan for the holidays in order to

options.A: Save Jimi from Wole's bad influence
options.B: Heed Jimi's request to stay with his friends
options.C: Allow Jimi spend some time with his friends
options.D: Allow Jimi to know more about Ibadan

subject: english
text: Choose the word that is most nearly opposite in meaning to the underlined word. At first we found life in the town very exciting but soon it became rather _______
explanation: 'Exciting' means causing great enthusiasm and eagerness; its opposite is 'boring', meaning not interesting or tedious.

subject: english
text: Suleiman's reason for becoming a Muslim in the novel is because Islam brings a sense of ______?
explanation: Suleiman was resolute about tracing his roots and finally finding his identity amongst his people in Africa and in the religion, Islam.

subject: english
text: Select the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence. The man's Achilles' heel is women ________
explanation: The phrase 'Achilles' heel' means a small problem or weakness in a person or system that can result in failure.

subject: english
text: Choose the most appropriate stress pattern from the options below. Constitution

subject: english
text: Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word(s) in italics. The driver was discharged while the car owner was ___________ for fraud.
explanation: To discharge someone means to legally release them from confinement; to detain means to put someone under custody.

subject: english
text: Choose the option nearest in meaning to the italicized words or expressions. The police later discovered that Audu feigned death.
explanation: To feign means to make a false show of something; to fake something means to make a false display.

subject: english
text: Select the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence. The events of last Friday show that there is no love lost between the Principal and his deputy.
explanation: The expression 'no love lost' means that the two parties involved never liked each other.

subject: english
text: Choose the option nearest in meaning to the italicized words or expressions. High mortality rate is becoming increasingly predominant.
explanation: 'Predominant' means common, significant and important; 'rampant' means common and unrestrained.

subject: english
text: Choose the option nearest in meaning to the italicized words or expressions. The rapist claimed that the woman's voluptuous hips attracted him.
explanation: 'Voluptuous' means full and attractive.

subject: english
text: Choose the word that has the same vowel sound as the one in the bracket. Neighb[our]

subject: english
text: Choose the word that has the same vowel sound as the one in the bracket. H[ei]r
explanation: The vowel sound here is the diphthong as in 'care' and 'air'.

subject: english
text: Choose the word that has the same consonant sound as the one in the bracket. A[d]just
explanation: The sound here is evidenced in 'journey' and 'gender'; 'judge' has the same consonant sound.

subject: english
text: Choose the word that has the same consonant sound as the one in the bracket. A[n]kle
explanation: The consonant sound here may be seen in 'gang' and 'sing'; 'tongue' has the same consonant sound.

subject: english
text: Choose the option that is nearest in meaning to the word(s) in the bracket. These policies have been [espoused] by the ruling party.
explanation: To espouse is to adopt or support a belief or strategy.

subject: english
text: Read the passage on theatre in traditional African society and answer the question. Theatre was popular in Africa because ____________

options.A: Was traditional and simple in nature
options.B: Dealt with the political, social, and economic problems of the society
options.D: Was communal and reflected the common concerns of the people

explanation: Paragraph one of the passage emphasizes community participation, peace and progress.

subject: english
text: Read the passage on theatre in traditional African society and answer the question. In the passage, the terms 'theatre' and 'drama' are used ____________
explanation: Throughout the passage, theatre and drama are used interchangeably; interchange means to switch or swap.

subject: english
text: Read the passage on theatre in traditional African society and answer the question. By the expression 'climb down', the author implies that modern playwrights should ____________

options.A: Be simplistic in the use of language
options.C: Use language that is most appreciated by the people
options.D: Wait for the community to catch up with them

explanation: In the passage 'climb down' implies to descend in the use of language to be understandable by the common man.

subject: english
text: Choose the option that best answers the question. He travels every WEEKEND.

options.B: Does he wash every week days?

explanation: Option C is correct because 'WEEKEND' contradicts the phrase 'every month'.

subject: english
text: Choose the option that best answers the question. He SLAPPED the teacher.
explanation: Option A is correct because 'SLAPPED' contradicts the word 'greet': Did he greet the teacher? No, he slapped the teacher.

subject: english
text: Choose the option that has the same consonant sound as the underlined letter(s). [B]ark

subject: english
text: Complete the statement with the correct order of adjectives. The manager met the _____________ who came visiting last week.
explanation: Order of adjectives: quantity, opinion, size, age, colour, shape, origin. Good-looking (opinion), tall (size), dark (colour), American (origin).

subject: english
text: Choose the option that has the same vowel sound as the underlined letter(s). M[oo]n

subject: english
text: Fill in the gap with the option that best completes the sentence. Between you and __________, he is a liar.
explanation: Use the pronoun 'me' when the person speaking is receiving the action of the verb, either directly or indirectly.

subject: english
text: Fill in the gap with the option that best completes the sentence. The secretary and treasurer did not do __________ job.
explanation: When two subjects are joined by 'and' but refer to only one person or thing, a singular verb (and singular possessive) should be used.

subject: english
text: Fill in the gap with the option that best completes the sentence. If James fails his examination, his teachers, his parents, his friends, or I __________ to be blamed.
explanation: With a list of subjects joined by 'or', it is the nearest subject to the verb that determines the choice of verb; 'I' takes 'am'.

subject: english
text: Fill in the gap with the option that best completes the sentence. I have always found that it is helpful to have a few wise and trusted people to __________ you can turn for information and advice.
explanation: 'Whom' should be used to refer to the object of a verb or preposition.

subject: english
text: Choose the word with the same vowel sound as the one in the bracket. Band[a]ge
explanation: The short vowel /i/ as in 'bit' is present here; only the last sound of 'cacophony' bears the same vowel.

subject: english
text: From the options lettered A-D, choose the option that is nearest in meaning to the underlined word. I have an aversion for war movies.
explanation: The word 'aversion' refers to a strong dislike or disinclination, so the nearest in meaning is 'dislike'.

subject: english
text: Choose the option that is nearest in meaning to the underlined word(s). When night fell, we had to hasten to the motor park.
explanation: 'Hasten' means to move or travel hurriedly; its synonyms include hurry, haste, dash and race.

subject: english
text: Choose the word that has the same rhyme scheme. laugh
explanation: The word 'laugh' rhymes with 'half'.

subject: english
text: Fill in the gap with the appropriate option. Children are not allowed in the park ...... dark.
explanation: The phrase 'after dark' means after nightfall; children are not permitted in the park during the nighttime.

subject: english
text: Fill the gap with the most appropriate option. We had to ..... the group headed by Taiwo.
explanation: 'Sell out' means to betray for money or reward; 'sell off' means to clear out stock at bargain prices; 'sell up' means to sell all the goods of a debtor.

subject: english
text: From the novel The Lekki Headmaster: What was the common ritual at the morning assembly at Stardom on Tuesdays and Thursdays?

options.C: The second stanza of the national anthem

explanation: On Tuesdays and Thursdays, Stardom Schools recited the second stanza of the national anthem ('Oh God of creation, direct our noble cause...'), found on page 6 of the novel.

subject: english
text: From the novel The Lekki Headmaster: How much was the boarding house fee per session at Stardom before it was reduced?

options.A: One hundred and sixty three thousand naira
options.B: Two hundred and fifty thousand naira
options.C: One hundred and sixty thousand naira

explanation: The boarding fee per session was N250,000 before it was reduced to N165,000, found on page 6 of the novel.
